description: Outcome-based persistent memory for AI coding tools (Claude Code, OpenCode)
release_notes: v0.5.3.1: Hotfix for the OpenCode plugin's JSON extraction logic — facts and summary lanes were silently failing on responses from thinking/reasoning models (greedy regex swallowing markdown code fences, non-greedy regex stopping at the first } inside nested memory_scores objects). Both lanes refactored onto a shared extractJson() helper that handles fences, nested objects, and embedded brace characters in one place. Bumped scoring max_tokens 2000 → 4000 and facts max_tokens 1000 → 2000 to give verbose reasoning models headroom. Verified end-to-end with a thinking sidecar model on LM Studio: multiple consecutive SUCCESS + SUMMARY stored + FACTS extracted with per-memory scoring, zero JSON Parse errors. Recommendation: prefer non-thinking sidecar models — thinking models can occasionally truncate output mid-reason. Also bundles a separate test-only commit consolidating Linux + Python 3.10 CI isolation fixes.
